verdict: High. Investment Analyst at Goldman Sachs scores 52 / 100 on AI displacement exposure over a 5-year horizon.

timeline.description: Recent trends in AI adoption in finance
Goldman Sachs' recent investments in AI-powered trading platforms

reasoning_steps.step: High exposure to routine cognitive tasks (financial modeling, data analysis) with significant potential for automation.
reasoning_steps.rationale: Recent trends in AI adoption in finance indicate increased automation of financial modeling and data analysis.

reasoning_steps.step: Significant potential for increased automation of investment research and recommendations due to Goldman Sachs' recent investments in AI-powered trading platforms.
reasoning_steps.rationale: Goldman Sachs' investments in AI-powered trading platforms suggest a high risk of automation in this role.

action_plan.step: Take courses in machine learning and data science to develop skills complementary to AI-powered finance tools.
action_plan.rationale: Developing skills in machine learning and data science can help mitigate the impact of automation on this role.

action_plan.step: Network with professionals in AI-powered finance to learn about emerging trends and opportunities.
action_plan.rationale: Staying informed about emerging trends and opportunities in AI-powered finance can help individuals adapt to changing job requirements.

follow_up_suggestions.suggestion: Research the impact of AI on investment research and recommendations in the financial industry.
follow_up_suggestions.description: Explore recent studies and reports on the effects of AI on investment analysis and decision-making.

follow_up_suggestions.suggestion: Attend a conference or workshop on AI-powered finance to learn from experts and network with professionals.
follow_up_suggestions.description: Stay up-to-date with the latest developments in AI-powered finance and learn about emerging trends and opportunities.

quotes.quote: AI is going to change every job. Every single job. Some jobs it will eliminate, some jobs it will improve, and some jobs it will create.
quotes.speaker: Jamie Dimon
quotes.title: CEO, JPMorgan Chase

quotes.quote: Two-thirds of jobs in the United States and Europe are exposed to some degree of AI automation, and roughly a quarter of current work could be done by generative AI.
quotes.speaker: Joseph Briggs and Devesh Kodnani
quotes.title: Economists, Goldman Sachs Research
quotes.source: Goldman Sachs report: The Potentially Large Effects of AI on Economic Growth

quotes.quote: Generative AI could add the equivalent of 2.6 to 4.4 trillion dollars in value annually across the global economy. Banking, high-tech, and life sciences capture the largest share.
quotes.speaker: McKinsey Global Institute
quotes.title: Research report
quotes.source: The Economic Potential of Generative AI

recent_developments.headline: Banks roll out AI copilots to analysts and traders
recent_developments.impact: JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley, and Goldman now offer LLM tools to thousands of frontline staff.

recent_developments.headline: Audit automation hits routine reconciliation work
recent_developments.impact: Big-Four firms report AI handling first-pass review on a growing share of engagements.

recent_developments.headline: Robo-advice AUM continues climbing
recent_developments.impact: Algorithmic platforms now manage over $1 trillion globally, pressuring entry-level advisory roles.
